projectGoal: Make all 341 official Hyprland scalar rows writable where possible, including officially supported variables/options within those settings, but only when official Hyprland documentation/source supports the behavior and the unified row-driven pipeline proves the row works safely.

candidateBuckets.readinessEvidence: Existing report marks display/render as best-next-candidate-as-small-subset-after-live-watchdog-process-proof and says it can split smaller.
candidateBuckets.knownBlocker: Needs independent watchdog process plus CLI/token confirmation; live execution remains disabled for this sprint.

candidateBuckets.readinessEvidence: Existing report says cursor/input should wait until input-independent confirmation is proven.
candidateBuckets.knownBlocker: Confirmation and recovery cannot depend on mouse, cursor visibility, Hyprland keybinds, or app UI.

candidateBuckets.readinessEvidence: Existing report says debug/crash should wait until crash-survivable watchdog is proven.
candidateBuckets.knownBlocker: Requires process survivability, persisted plan ownership, and crash/failure result handling before enablement.

candidateBuckets.readinessEvidence: Fallback option when existing reports do not prove enough detail to choose a specific bucket.
candidateBuckets.knownBlocker: Not needed because existing reports provide a specific display/render grouped readiness candidate.

selectedNextBatch.name: non-enabling display/render readiness inventory for non-display-critical boolean/finite-choice candidates
selectedNextBatch.batchScope: Use the existing display/render blocked-row reports to identify a small candidate batch, but do not enable rows until a later sprint proves row-specific source, validator, gate, and recovery evidence.

selectionReason: Existing project evidence identifies display/render as the best next grouped candidate because it can split smaller and has likely validators available. Cursor/input and debug/crash remain less ready because their reports require input-independent or crash-survivable recovery proof before enablement. This sprint selects only a non-enabling readiness batch.
batchingPolicy: Use grouped high-risk bucket planning. Prefer a small display/render subset with non-display-critical boolean or finite-choice validators. Do not isolate a single row unless reports prove a single row must be isolated for safety.
avoidRabbitHolePolicy: Do not continue decoration.screen_shader work by default. Do not start one-row deep dives unless explicitly approved or required by a proven safety failure.
sourceEvidencePolicy: Future batch rows require official Hyprland documentation/source evidence for the setting, value shape, runtime consumer, and any side effects before enablement can be considered.
writeEnablementPolicy: This sprint enables no rows. Future enablement must occur only after source proof, validator proof, high-risk gate proof, watchdog/recovery proof, fixture tests, and aggregate report reconciliation.
proofRequiredBeforeEnablement: row-specific official source or docs proof
row-specific value parser and validator proof
write planning and apply-flow gate proof when risky
watchdog or recovery proof that does not rely on the failed UI path
aggregate report reconciliation with unchanged unrelated behavior
explicit approval for any row enablement sprint
riskGatePolicy: Risky display/render rows must remain blocked until a source-backed gate policy and tests prove they cannot bypass required review/recovery boundaries.
watchdogRecoveryPolicy: Watchdog/recovery proof is required before high-risk display/render row enablement. Existing watchdog migration proof for screen_shader does not automatically apply to other rows.
uiWarningPolicy: Future UI warnings may support user understanding, but UI metadata or warnings alone do not prove write safety and cannot enable a row.
advisoryPolicy: Advisory checks may be useful as non-authoritative evidence, but advisory helper/UI/tool output cannot replace source-backed validators, gates, watchdogs, or recovery proof.
testsRequiredForFutureEnablement: counts remain 341 readable / 278 writable / 63 blocked until an approved enablement sprint
SAFE_WRITABLE_ROWS length remains unchanged until approved enablement
write allowlist unchanged until approved enablement
selected batch rows have source-backed value and consumer proof
selected batch rows have validator acceptance and rejection tests
risk gate tests reject ungated high-risk writes
watchdog/recovery tests prove rollback or survivability boundaries
unrelated writable rows remain on the normal path
doNotInferSafetyFrom: policy name without source-backed evidence and tests
nextRecommendedSprint: Display/render blocked rows readiness batching sprint
proofStillMissing: exact display/render row list for a future readiness batch
row-specific official source proof for each future selected display/render row
row-specific validator proof for each future selected display/render row
bucket-specific display/render gate proof for future selected rows
watchdog/recovery proof for future selected display/render rows
explicit approval for any future row enablement sprint
